ddls:
CREATE TABLE "covid_testing_data" (
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each record */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the reported data */,
  "daily_change_total" INTEGER /* Daily change in cumulative total of tests */,
  "positive_rate" REAL /* Positive rate of tests conducted */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country where the data was collected */,
  "units" TEXT /* Units of measurement used for tests (e.g., tests performed) */,
  "source_url" TEXT /* URL to the source of the data */,
  "source_label" TEXT /* Label indicating the source of the data */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes regarding the data entry */,
  "test_type" TEXT /* Type of test conducted (e.g., PCR, antigen) */,
  "total_tests" INTEGER /* Cumulative total of tests conducted */,
  "positive_cases" INTEGER /* Cumulative total of positive cases */,
  "test_frequency_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the reporting frequency */,
  "data_quality_score" REAL /* Score indicating the quality of the data */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version number of the data */,
  "last_modified_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who last modified the record */,
  "last_modified_date" TEXT /* Date of the last modification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("record_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_covid_testing_data_source_url FOREIGN KEY ("source_url") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_url"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_covid_testing_data_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_covid_testing_data_test_frequency_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_frequency_id") REFERENCES reporting_frequencies ("frequency_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_covid_testing_data_units FOREIGN KEY ("units") REFERENCES testing_units ("unit_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "iso_code" TEXT /* ISO code of the country for standardization */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the country */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region to which the country belongs */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_url" TEXT /* URL of the data source */,
  "source_label" TEXT /* Label describing the data source */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data source and its credibility */,
  "last_update" TEXT /* Date of the last update */,
  "data_source_type" TEXT /* Type of data source (e.g., government, research institute) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "testing_units" (
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each unit type */,
  "unit_name" TEXT /* Name of the unit (e.g., tests performed) */,
  "unit_description" TEXT /* Description of the unit */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reporting_frequencies" (
  "frequency_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each reporting frequency */,
  "frequency_name" TEXT /* Name of the frequency (e.g., daily, weekly) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the reporting frequency */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("frequency_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_quality_checks" (
  "check_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each quality check */,
  "record_id" INTEGER /* ID of the record being checked */,
  "check_date" TEXT /* Date the quality check was performed */,
  "check_result" TEXT /* Result of the quality check (e.g., passed, failed) */,
  "review_notes" TEXT /* Notes regarding the quality check */,
  "checker_id" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who performed the check */,
  "check_method" TEXT /* Method used for the quality check */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("check_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_quality_checks_checker_id FOREIGN KEY ("checker_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_quality_checks_record_id FOREIGN KEY ("record_id") REFERENCES covid_testing_data ("record_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., data analyst, public health official) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level of the user (e.g., read, write, admin) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
